prompt:
Revenue reporting team. Our account roll-up job still screen-scrapes a weekly CSV export from Salesforce and it broke twice this quarter. I want a proper JVM client for the Salesforce GraphQL API that walks the whole Account result set and hands us typed records. Plain Java, stdlib only (java.net.http is fine) — there's a small Json.java helper in the repo you can use or replace.

Do the homework first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Salesforce GraphQL API guide on developer.salesforce.com — the requests-and-responses page, the query-records/object pages, the record-connection pagination page, and the status-codes/error-responses page. Confirm the current stable API version and versioned endpoint path, the POST body parts (query/variables), the uiapi connection shape (edges/node/cursor, pageInfo, totalCount), the first/after cursor contract, how GraphQL errors ride alongside partial data in a 200, and how field-level security shows up in the schema and in null field values. Our reading of those docs is pinned in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — protected fixtures, like the test harness. If the live official docs contradict them, flag that loudly in your summary and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually fetched in your final message.

Deliverable (split it sensibly — client / exporter / records / errors; TestMain.java pins only this surface):

- new AccountsExporter(String instanceUrl, String apiVersion, String accessToken, int pageSize) with AccountsExport fetchAll() throws GraphqlHttpException, GraphqlQueryException.
- AccountsExport: public List<AccountRecord> records, public long totalCount, public List<GraphqlError> warnings. AccountRecord: public String id, name, industry, website and public Double annualRevenue — decoded from Id plus the Name/Industry/AnnualRevenue/Website field-value objects; a value of null stays null (that's an empty field per the FLS docs, not an excuse to drop the record).
- One GraphQL operation document, built once: variables carry the page size and cursor ($first/$after) — the document must be byte-identical across pages and must never contain a cursor literal. Follow pageInfo (hasNextPage/endCursor) until the scan is done; records come back in edge order, each exactly once; totalCount surfaces on the export.
- Error discipline, straight from the researched docs: a 200 with errors AND data keeps the data and exposes the errors as warnings (GraphqlError: public String message, public String errorType from the classification, public List<Object> path). A 200 with null data and errors raises GraphqlQueryException carrying those GraphqlErrors. A non-200 (401 session death, 404 wrong endpoint/version) raises GraphqlHttpException with public int status and public String errorCode decoded from the REST error array. The access token must never appear in any exception text.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java (it spins up a loopback fake org — no real Salesforce, no real credentials). TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Salesforce GraphQL API, POST /services/data/v67.0/graphql (Summer '26, API version 67.0)

official_sources.title: Requests and Responses | Schema Structure | GraphQL API
official_sources.used_for: endpoint URL form https://{MyDomainName}.my.salesforce.com/services/data/v{version}/graphql; POST JSON body parts query (required string), operationName, variables, extensions; response sections data, errors, extensions

official_sources.title: Query Objects | Query Records | GraphQL API
official_sources.used_for: uiapi wrapper with a query RecordQuery root; object fields returning connection types (AccountConnection); RecordEdge with node and cursor; field-value types where every accessible field exposes a value field; Record interface scalars such as Id

official_sources.title: Paginate with Record Connections | Query Records | GraphQL API
official_sources.used_for: first argument bounding page size (default 10), after argument taking a cursor, pageInfo.endCursor continuation, cursor-per-edge, and passing endCursor as after while hasNextPage is true

official_sources.title: Status Codes and Error Responses | Get Started | GraphQL API
official_sources.used_for: GraphQL API returns 200 OK even when the request contains errors; 401 for expired/invalid session; 404 when a non-graphql path is used; GraphQLErrorRepresentation ErrorType values InvalidSyntax, ValidationError, DataFetchingException, OperationNotSupported, ExecutionAborted

official_sources.title: What is GraphQL API? | Get Started | GraphQL API
official_sources.used_for: the schema exposes UI API enabled sObjects honoring the context user's object- and field-level security, so two users can see different schemas

research.language_rationale: The consuming revenue-reporting service is a JVM batch job; Salesforce documents the GraphQL API as a raw HTTP POST protocol without an official Java SDK, so java.net.http.HttpClient against the documented endpoint is the credible zero-dependency integration surface.
